fields.name: api_key
fields.title: Access Token
fields.description: Your Shopify Admin API access token

fields.name: client_id
fields.title: Client ID
fields.description: Your Shopify OAuth2 application client ID

fields.name: client_secret
fields.title: Client Secret

fields.name: access_token
fields.title: Access Token
fields.description: Your Shopify OAuth2 access token

custom_description:
Shopify is an e-commerce platform that enables businesses to create online stores,
manage products, process orders, and handle customer relationships. This connector
provides access to Shopify Admin REST API for reading store data including customers,
orders, products, inventory, and more.
supported_questions:
- List all customers in my Shopify store
- Show me details for a recent customer
- What products do I have in my store?
- List all locations for my store
- Show me inventory levels for a recent location
- Show me all draft orders
- List all custom collections in my store
- Show me details for a recent order
- Show me product variants for a recent product
- Create a new customer in Shopify with email test@example.com
- Update a customer's first name
- Create a new product called 'Summer T-Shirt'
- Update a product title
- Create a draft order for a customer
- Complete a draft order
- Set inventory quantity for a product variant at a location
- Create a basic discount code for 10% off
- Set a metafield on a product
- Create a new page on my store
- Create a new blog post
- Show me orders from the last 30 days
- Show me abandoned checkouts from this week
- What price rules are currently active?
- Show me all pages on my store
- List all blog articles
- Are there any open disputes?
unsupported_questions:
- Process a refund
- Send shipping notification to customer
- Modify order line items after creation

customers.description:
Creates a new customer in the store via GraphQL mutation.
Requires at least one of: email, phone, firstName, or lastName.

schema.description: CustomerInput object with id

id.description: The GraphQL GID of the customer (e.g. gid://shopify/Customer/123)

tags.description: Tags to associate with the customer

taxExempt.description: Whether the customer is tax exempt

parameters.description: CustomerInput object with id

customers.description:
Updates an existing customer via GraphQL mutation.
All fields except id are optional for partial updates.

id.description: The GraphQL GID of the customer to delete (e.g. gid://shopify/Customer/123)

customers.description:
Deletes a customer from the store via GraphQL mutation.
Only succeeds if the customer has no orders. This action is irreversible.

to add additional variants afterwards.

parameters.name: variants

schema.description: List of variants to create

inventoryItem.description: Inventory-item attributes for the variant. As of Admin API 2024-07+ the SKU is no longer a variant-level field and must be set here (a top-level sku is ignored/rejected by productVariantsBulkCreate).

optionValues.description: Required in practice: each variant must supply one option value for EVERY option defined on the product. A product with options Color and Size needs both here; a product with only the default option needs a single "Title" entry. Omitting an option (or optionValues entirely) causes a userErrors rejection.

optionName.description: Name of the product option this value belongs to (e.g. "Color", "Size", or "Title" for a single-option product).

name.description: The option value (e.g. "Large"). If the value already exists on the option it is linked; otherwise a new option value is created.

parameters.description: List of variants to create

product_variants.description:
Creates one or more product variants via GraphQL mutation.
Variants are created in bulk for a given product.

parameters.name: productId

schema.description: The GraphQL GID of the product

parameters.description: The GraphQL GID of the product

parameters.name: variants

schema.description: List of variants to update (each must include id)

id.description: The GraphQL GID of the variant

inventoryItem.description: Inventory-item attributes for the variant. As of Admin API 2024-07+ the SKU is no longer a variant-level field and must be set here (a top-level sku is ignored/rejected by productVariantsBulkUpdate, which silently returns null instead of updating).

parameters.description: List of variants to update (each must include id)

product_variants.description:
Updates one or more product variants via GraphQL mutation.
Variants are updated in bulk for a given product.

name.description: Author display name. Required (provide the author's name).

schema.description: The GraphQL GID of the draft order to complete

parameters.description: The GraphQL GID of the draft order to complete

parameters.name: paymentPending

schema.description: Whether payment is pending (true) or mark as paid (false/omit)

parameters.description: Whether payment is pending (true) or mark as paid (false/omit)

draft_order_complete.description: Completes a draft order, converting it to a regular order via GraphQL mutation.

inventoryItemId.description: GraphQL GID of the inventory item

inventory_set.description:
Sets absolute inventory quantities for items at locations via GraphQL mutation.
Uses the inventorySetQuantities mutation with a required reason and reference document.

inventoryItemId.description: GraphQL GID of the inventory item

delta.description: Amount to adjust (positive to add, negative to subtract)

inventory_adjust.description: Adjusts inventory quantities relatively (add/subtract) for items at locations via GraphQL mutation.

parameters.name: metafields

schema.description: List of metafields to set

ownerId.description: GraphQL GID of the owner resource (e.g. gid://shopify/Product/123)

parameters.description: List of metafields to set

metafields.description:
Sets (creates or updates) up to 25 metafields atomically via GraphQL mutation.
Works across all resource types (products, customers, orders, etc.).

parameters.name: metafields

schema.description: List of metafield identifiers to delete

ownerId.description: GraphQL GID of the owner resource (e.g. gid://shopify/Product/123)

parameters.description: List of metafield identifiers to delete

metafields.description:
Deletes one or more metafields via GraphQL mutation.
Identifies metafields by ownerId + namespace + key.

fields.description: Unique identifier for the article

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the article

fields.name: handle
fields.description: URL-friendly handle for the article

fields.name: author
fields.description: Name of the author of the article

fields.name: blog_id
fields.description: Identifier of the blog the article belongs to

fields.name: body_html
fields.description: HTML content of the article body

fields.name: summary_html
fields.description: Summary of the article in HTML

fields.name: tags
fields.description: Comma-separated list of tags for the article

fields.name: published_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the article was published

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the article was created

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the article was last updated

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier of the balance transaction

fields.name: type
fields.description: Type of the transaction (charge, refund, dispute, reserve, adjustment, credit, debit, payout, etc.)

fields.name: amount
fields.description: Gross amount of the transaction

fields.name: fee
fields.description: Total fees deducted from the transaction

fields.name: net
fields.description: Net amount of the transaction

fields.name: currency
fields.description: ISO 4217 currency code of the transaction

fields.name: payout_id
fields.description: Identifier of the payout the transaction was paid out in

fields.name: payout_status
fields.description: Status of the associated payout

fields.name: source_type
fields.description: Type of the resource that led to this transaction

fields.name: source_order_id
fields.description: Identifier of the source order, if applicable

fields.name: processed_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the transaction was processed

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the dispute

fields.name: order_id
fields.description: Identifier of the order the dispute belongs to

fields.name: type
fields.description: Whether the dispute is an inquiry or chargeback

fields.name: amount
fields.description: Disputed amount

fields.name: currency
fields.description: ISO 4217 currency code of the dispute amount

fields.name: reason
fields.description: Reason for the dispute provided by the cardholder's bank

fields.name: network_reason_code
fields.description: Network reason code from the cardholder's bank

fields.name: status
fields.description: Current state of the dispute (needs_response, under_review, charge_refunded, accepted, won, lost)

fields.name: evidence_due_by
fields.description: ISO 8601 deadline for evidence submission

fields.name: initiated_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the dispute was initiated

fields.name: finalized_on
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the dispute was resolved

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the metafield

fields.name: namespace
fields.description: Container namespace for the metafield

fields.name: key
fields.description: Identifier key for the metafield

fields.name: value
fields.description: The metafield value

fields.name: type
fields.description: The metafield's information type

fields.name: description
fields.description: Human-readable description of the metafield

fields.name: owner_id
fields.description: Identifier of the page that owns this metafield

fields.name: owner_resource
fields.description: Resource type that owns this metafield (e.g. `page`)

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the metafield was created

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the metafield was last updated
